 {"id":"bd-1w78","title":"[13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 13

Task Objective:
Instrument and enforce continuous lockstep validation for compatibility claims.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1w78/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1w78/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Lockstep validation runs continuously: every CI pipeline execution includes a lockstep comparison against reference Node.js on a representative test subset.
2. The representative subset covers >= 100 API calls across critical API families (fs, http, crypto, stream, net).
3. Any lockstep divergence fails CI and produces a divergence receipt with: input, expected output, actual output, API family, severity.
4. Lockstep validation history is tracked: a dashboard or report shows pass/fail trend over the last 30 days.
5. Divergence receipts are immutable once created (append-only storage).
6. Lockstep validation can be triggered on-demand for a specific API family (not just full suite).
7. Evidence: lockstep_validation_status.json with latest run date, pass rate, and list of any open divergences.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:34.389711289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T23:10:09.598558027Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T23:10:09.598504697Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w78","depends_on_id":"bd-2f43","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:25.313179869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1wpq","title":"Fix certification registry stale evaluation after promote/demote","description":"Fresh-eyes audit found that crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs updates CertificationRecord.level during promote/demote but leaves CertificationRecord.evaluation stale. Downstream readers can observe conflicting certification levels from the same record. Fix the registry to keep evaluation metadata consistent with promoted/demoted state and add focused regressions.","notes":"Patched `crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs` so `CertificationRegistry::promote()` and `demote()` refresh the embedded `CertificationRecord.evaluation` snapshot instead of leaving level/max_achievable/explanation stale. Added focused inline regressions for both promotion and demotion.

Validation history:
- prior `rch` targeted test attempt stalled in shared external dependency compilation without surfacing a source failure
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs` PASS
- `git diff --check -- crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs` PASS
- `rch check` PASS
- 2026-03-12 CloudyAnchor retried two isolated remote validation lanes on fresh workers/target dirs:
  - `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/home/ubuntu CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cloudyanchor_bd1wpq RCH_BUILD_TIMEOUT=900 cargo test --manifest-path crates/franken-node/Cargo.toml refreshes_embedded_evaluation_snapshot --lib -- --nocapture`
    - worker `vmi1156319`; progressed through sync + deep dependency compile, then entered recurring `progress_stale` execute state with no source error or green verdict surfaced back to the shell
  - `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/home/ubuntu CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cloudyanchor_bd1wpq_check RCH_BUILD_TIMEOUT=900 cargo check --manifest-path crates/franken-node/Cargo.toml -p frankenengine-node --lib`
    - worker `vmi1153651`; likewise progressed through sync + dependency checking, then entered `progress_stale` execute state with no source error or green verdict surfaced back to the shell
- Both lanes were canceled by CloudyAnchor after reproducing the same infrastructure-stall mode on isolated workers; no local cargo fallback was accepted.

Current state: file-local fix appears landed and stable, but trustworthy remote cargo verification is still infrastructure-blocked, so bead was returned to `open` for the next available closeout pass rather than being over-claimed as verified.

2026-03-12 CloudySparrow closeout pass:
- Source review confirms the intended fix is already present in `crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs`: `promote()` and `demote()` both call `refresh_manual_evaluation()` so `record.evaluation.level` and explanation stay aligned with manual level changes while evidence-derived fields remain preserved.
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs` PASS
- `git diff --check -- crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs` PASS
- Fresh `rch` retry initially failed because worker-local `/tmp` target dirs were full (`No space left on device` writing `.fingerprint/.../invoked.timestamp`).
- Retried with project-scoped remote target dirs (`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-tmp/...`, `TMPDIR=/data/tmp`) to avoid `/tmp` exhaustion.
- Rerouted `rch` validation still remained infrastructure-blocked:
  - `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-tmp/target_cloudysparrow_bd1wpq_check RCH_BUILD_TIMEOUT=1200 cargo check --manifest-path crates/franken-node/Cargo.toml -p frankenengine-node --lib`
    - worker `vmi1153651`; progressed through sync/compile, then ended `Remote command finished: exit=137 in 301642ms` with the build classified as `progress_stale`.
  - `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-tmp/target_cloudysparrow_bd1wpq_tests RCH_BUILD_TIMEOUT=1200 cargo test --manifest-path crates/franken-node/Cargo.toml -p frankenengine-node refreshes_embedded_evaluation_snapshot --lib -- --nocapture`
    - worker `vmi1153651`; remained stuck in `sync_up` / `progress_stale` without surfacing a source error or a passing verdict back to the shell.
- Current assessment: this bead no longer looks blocked by `certification.rs` correctness. It remains blocked on trustworthy remote cargo evidence from `rch`, so I am returning it to `open` rather than falsely closing it.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:51:26.052117244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:13:34.677808652Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1wz","title":"[PLAN 10.17] Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)","description":"Section: 10.17 — Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)

Strategic Context:
Radical expansion track for proof-carrying speculation, Bayesian adversary control, time-travel replay, ZK attestations, and claim compiler systems.

Execution Requirements:
- Preserve all scoped capabilities from the canonical plan.
- Keep contracts self-contained so future contributors can execute without reopening the master plan.
- Require explicit testing strategy (unit + integration/e2e) and structured logging/telemetry for every child bead.
- Require artifact-backed validation for performance, security, and correctness claims.

Dependency Semantics:
- This epic is blocked by its child implementation beads.
- Cross-epic dependencies encode strategic sequencing and canonical ownership boundaries.

## Success Criteria
- All child beads for this section are completed with acceptance artifacts attached and no unresolved blockers.
- Section-level verification gate for comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e workflows, and detailed structured logging evidence is green.
- Scope-to-plan traceability is explicit, with no feature loss versus the canonical plan section.

## Optimization Notes
- User-Outcome Lens: "[PLAN 10.17] Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)" must improve operator confidence, safety posture, and deterministic recovery behavior under both normal and adversarial conditions.
- Cross-Section Coordination: child beads must encode integration assumptions explicitly to avoid local optimizations that degrade system-wide correctness.
- Verification Non-Negotiable: completion requires reproducible unit/integration/E2E evidence and structured logs suitable for independent replay.
- Scope Protection: any simplification must preserve canonical-plan feature intent and be justified with explicit tradeoff documentation.","notes":"Acceptance Criteria alias (plan-space normalization, 2026-02-20):
- The `Success Criteria` section in this bead is the canonical acceptance contract for closure.
- Closure additionally requires linked unit/integration/E2E verification evidence and detailed structured logging artifacts from all child implementation  {"id":"bd-1xao","title":"[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 13

Task Objective:
Track and gate production-grade adoption of impossible-by-default capabilities.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1xao/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1xao/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. 'Impossible-by-default' capabilities are defined: operations that are blocked unless explicitly enabled with cryptographic authorization.
2. At minimum, these capabilities are impossible-by-default: (a) arbitrary file system access outside project root, (b) outbound network to non-allowlisted hosts, (c) spawning child processes without sandbox, (d) loading unsigned extensions, (e) disabling hardening profiles.
3. Each impossible-by-default capability requires explicit opt-in via signed capability token with expiry.
4. Attempting a blocked operation produces a clear, actionable error message (not a generic permission denied).
5. Adoption metric: >= 90% of production deployments run with all impossible-by-default capabilities enforced (measured via telemetry).
6. No impossible-by-default capability can be silently disabled; any disabling is logged and alerted.
7. Evidence: capability_enforcement_report.json listing each capability, enforcement status, and opt-in rate.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:34.668279352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T23:37:02.590071518Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T23:37:02.590043385Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xao","depends_on_id":"bd-pga7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:25.443838167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xbc","title":"[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.17 — Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)

Why This Exists:
Radical expansion track for proof-carrying speculation, Bayesian adversary control, time-travel replay, ZK attestations, and claim compiler systems.

Task Objective:
Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Captured executions replay byte-for-byte equivalent control decisions under same seed/input; incident replay includes stepwise state navigation and divergence explanation.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/time_travel_runtime.md`, `src/replay/time_travel_engine.rs`, `tests/lab/time_travel_replay_equivalence.rs`, `artifacts/10.17/time_travel_replay_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-1xbc/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-1xbc/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.

 {"id":"bd-qlc6","title":"[10.14] Map remote/control tasks to lane-aware scheduler classes with priority policies.","description":"## Why This Exists

The 9J enhancement maps require that all control-plane and remote-effect workloads run under explicit scheduling discipline to prevent priority inversion, lane starvation, and uncontrolled resource consumption. Without lane-aware scheduling, a flood of low-priority remote tasks can starve critical epoch transitions or marker writes, violating runtime invariant #7 (epoch barriers must complete within bounded time). This bead establishes the foundational scheduler classification that all downstream lane-policy consumers (10.15 lane mapping, 10.11 product integration) depend on. It directly supports invariant #9 (deterministic verification gates) by making task classification auditable and telemetry-backed.

## What This Must Do

1. Define a `SchedulerLane` enum covering at minimum: `ControlCritical`, `RemoteEffect`, `Maintenance`, `Background`, each with configurable priority weight and concurrency cap.
2. Implement a `LaneMappingPolicy` that maps incoming task types (identified by `TaskClass` discriminant) to scheduler lanes based on declarative policy configuration.
3. Enforce lane starvation detection: if any lane has zero completions within a configurable time window while tasks are queued, emit a `LANE_STARVED` alert event with lane ID, queue depth, and elapsed time.
4. Enforce misclassification detection: reject tasks whose `TaskClass` does not match any policy rule and emit `LANE_MISCLASS` event with task metadata.
5. Expose lane telemetry counters: `lane_active_count`, `lane_queued_count`, `lane_completed_total`, `lane_starvation_events`, per-lane latency histograms.
6. Produce a spec document defining the lane model, priority semantics, starvation thresholds, and configuration schema.
7. Produce a conformance test that validates lane assignment correctness, starvation detection firing, and misclassification rejection under controlled load.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Task classes are mapped to lanes by policy; lane starvation and misclassification checks are enforced; lane telemetry is exposed.
- Given 4+ task classes, each maps to exactly one lane per policy; no task class maps to zero lanes.
- Starvation detection fires within 2x the configured starvation window when a lane is blocked.
- Misclassification rejection returns a typed error with the unrecognized `TaskClass` value.
- Telemetry counters increment correctly under concurrent load (verified via test harness).
- Policy changes take effect without restart (hot-reload path exists and is tested).

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests: lane assignment for each task class; starvation timer triggers on blocked lane; misclassification rejection for unknown task class; policy reload applies new mapping.
- Integration tests: concurrent multi-lane load with starvation injection; lane fairness under skewed workloads; telemetry accuracy under >1000 tasks.
- Conformance tests: `tests/conformance/lane_mapping_enforcement.rs` -- normative test covering all policy rules and edge cases.
- Structured logs: `LANE_ASSIGN` (task_id, task_class, lane_id), `LANE_STARVED` (lane_id, queue_depth, elapsed_ms), `LANE_MISCLASS` (task_class, rejection_reason), `LANE_METRICS` (periodic telemetry snapshot). All events carry trace correlation IDs.

## Expected Artifacts

- `docs/specs/lane_mapping_policy.md` -- specification of lane model, priority semantics, configuration schema
- `tests/conformance/lane_mapping_enforcement.rs` -- normative conformance tests
- `artifacts/10.14/lane_mapping_metrics.csv` -- telemetry snapshot from conformance run
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-qlc6/verification_evidence.json` -- machine-readable CI/release gate evidence
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-qlc6/verification_summary.md` -- human-readable verification summary

## Dependencies

- Upstream: bd-v4l0 (global remote bulkhead with `remote_max_in_flight`) -- provides the concurrency envelope this bead subdivides into lanes.
- Downstream: bd-3epz (section-wide verification gate), bd-cuut (10.15 lane mapping for control-plane workloads), bd-lus (10.11 product integration of scheduler lanes), bd-5rh (section gate).","acceptance_criteria":"Task classes are mapped to lanes by policy; lane starvation and misclassification checks are enforced; lane telemetry is exposed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:58.061700429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:44:37.220499422Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:44:37.220471490Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-14","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qlc6","depends_on_id":"bd-v4l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:15.698361701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-qlc6.1","title":"[bd-qlc6] Detect queued-lane starvation before first completion","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` only reports starvation when `last_completion_ms` is `Some(...)`. That means a lane that immediately saturates, queues additional work, and then stays blocked past the starvation window is treated as non-starved forever until the first completion happens. `tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs::starvation_detected_when_lane_idle_too_long` expects the opposite behavior: queued Background work should be reported as starved after the window elapses even if the lane has never completed a task yet.

What this bug must do:
- make starvation detection use a stable baseline for lanes with queued work but no completion history yet
- keep the implementation fail-closed and deterministic
- update inline/unit/conformance coverage so the never-completed queued case is treated as starved after the configured window instead of silently ignored

Acceptance criteria:
1. `LaneScheduler::check_starvation` reports starvation for a queued lane after the configured window even if the lane has never completed a task.
2. The scheduler records accurate starvation telemetry and audit evidence for that case.
3. Inline tests in `runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` and the existing conformance expectation in `tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs` agree on the same behavior.
4. Validation uses `rch` for cargo-heavy checks only.","notes":"Implemented in `crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs`:\n- added `first_queued_at_ms` to track when queued work began waiting\n- starvation now uses the queued-work baseline even before any completion history exists\n- completion resets the queued baseline when work remains queued\n- inline tests now cover pre-window no-starvation, queued-before-first-completion starvation, and audit evidence\n\nVerification status:\n- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` PASS\n- `git diff --check -- crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` PASS\n- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_rusticdune_bd_qlc6_1 cargo check --all-targets` PASS for `frankenengine-node`; dependency warnings remain in `/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/control_plane_mock_inventory.rs`\n- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_rusticdune_bd_qlc6_1 cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` blocked by pre-existing unrelated lints in `crates/franken-node/src/connector/canonical_serializer.rs` and `crates/franken-node/src/connector/crdt.rs`\n- targeted `rch` cargo test lanes are still running/stalling in the shared worker fleet; no green test verdict captured yet","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:52:22.105655019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:38:00.397447161Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","scheduler","self-contained"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qlc6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-qlc6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:52:22.105655019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-qlc6.1","author":"FrostyPond","text":"Closeout pass on 2026-03-12: added Cargo-visible wrapper crates/franken-node/tests/control_lane_policy.rs for existing root conformance spec tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs. First remote conformance run via rch (build 29750734287274312 on vmi1293453) no longer failed with missing test target; instead it surfaced real Rust compile errors in the conformance spec: unused LaneMappingPolicy import and E0502 immutable audit_log() borrows held across sched.complete_task() calls. Fixed those in tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs, then validated file-locally with rustfmt --edition 2024 tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs crates/franken-node/tests/control_lane_policy.rs and git diff --check -- tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs crates/franken-node/tests/control_lane_policy.rs. Second remote rerun via rch (build 29750734287274319 on vmi1156319) progressed through compile but degraded into the shared fleet stall pattern; after several minutes with no new progress it was canceled explicitly to avoid wasting slots. Net result: the bead is improved materially (conformance target is now Cargo-visible and the exposed compile blockers are fixed), but trustworthy green rch cargo evidence is still outstanding.","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:37:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qrqs1","title":"Fresh-eyes audit of recent agent Rust fixes","description":"Review recent agent-written changes in crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs and crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs plus adjacent tests and call sites; diagnose any logic, reliability, or edge-case bugs from first principles; fix root causes; validate with rch-offloaded cargo checks/tests as needed; report findings to Agent Mail.","notes":"2026-03-12 audit pass findings:
- Patched crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs so manual promote/demote updates evaluation.level + explanation without fabricating evidence-derived satisfied/unsatisfied criteria. Preserves max_achievable and derivation metadata.
- Patched crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs to latch starvation state, emit one LANE_STARVED event per starvation episode, and emit LANE_STARVATION_CLEARED on recovery while still returning current starvation status from check_starvation().
- Patched crates/franken-node/src/main.rs registry request builders to fail closed when provenance link signing fails and to derive provenance.vcs_commit_sha with a bounded prefix helper instead of panic-prone `[..12]` slicing on caller-provided hashes. Added a short-hash regression test.

Validation:
- rustfmt --edition 2024 on touched files PASS
- git diff --check on touched files PASS
- python3 -m pytest tests/test_check_activation_pipeline.py tests/test_check_lane_scheduler.py -q PASS (76 passed)
- `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/home/ubuntu/.rch-tmp/foggysnow_qrqs1_main CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/ubuntu/.rch-cargo-targets/foggysnow_qrqs1_main_check cargo check -p frankenengine-node --bin frankenengine-node` PASS (remote worker vmi1293453, exit 0).
- Focused `rch` cargo test for `build_registry_publish_request_handles_short_hashes_without_panicking` did not reach franken_node test execution because sibling crate `frankenengine-engine` currently fails to compile with unrelated move/type errors in `module_cache.rs`, `offline_synthesis_pipeline.rs`, `regexp_deterministic_engine.rs`, `replacement_lineage_log.rs`, and `succinct_witness_compiler.rs`.
- rch health was otherwise OK (8/8 workers healthy) but multiple workers show storage pressure.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggySnow","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:46:09.278591874Z","created_by":"FoggySnow","updated_at":"2026-03-12T02:43:25.341125724Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T02:43:25.341100387Z","close_reason":"Completed audit pass; fixes landed, residual cargo-test blocker is unrelated sibling frankenengine-engine compile breakage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","review","rust"]}
